sources.writer_profile: Sara Riobom is a food and travel writer who runs Portoalities, a comprehensive local guide to Porto. Her neighbourhood-by-neighbourhood restaurant recommendations draw on direct personal visits and deep local knowledge of the city's dining scene.

Leonor Tito is a Porto-based writer who created Go Ask A Local, a personal dining and culture guide to Porto. She provides insider recommendations from a local resident's perspective, covering authentic restaurants and experiences across the city's neighbourhoods.

Florbela Alves and Lucília Monteiro are food and culture journalists at Visão, one of Portugal's leading weekly news magazines. Their restaurant coverage tracks new openings and culinary developments across Portugal, including Porto's evolving dining scene.

Rafael Tonon is a Brazilian-born food writer and journalist based in Portugal, contributing to Culinary Backstreets, an editorial food travel platform specialising in deep local food culture reporting. He has written extensively about Porto's culinary landscape and contemporary Portuguese cuisine.

restaurants.name: Taberna dos Mercadores

sources.quote: You can choose some starters (the fish soup is delicious) and then try a fish or meat dish; my favorite dish is perhaps the salted sea bass, which comes straight from the grill to the table.

restaurants.quote: You can choose some starters (the fish soup is delicious) and then try a fish or meat dish; my favorite dish is perhaps the salted sea bass, which comes straight from the grill to the table.

restaurants.name: Tapabento

sources.quote: Personally, I really liked the wild shrimp risotto (23€) and the fish curry (24€), but the starters are also excellent.

restaurants.quote: Personally, I really liked the wild shrimp risotto (23€) and the fish curry (24€), but the starters are also excellent.

restaurants.name: Postigo do Carvão

sources.quote: a very varied menu that features various cod dishes, fresh hake fillets (a rarity in the center of Porto!) and feijoada transmontana (a bean stew from the northeast of Portugal)

restaurants.quote: a very varied menu that features various cod dishes, fresh hake fillets (a rarity in the center of Porto!) and feijoada transmontana (a bean stew from the northeast of Portugal)

restaurants.name: Escondidinho do Barredo

sources.quote: I usually like to order some codfish cakes (they are the best in town!), octopus salad with green sauce, moelinhas (chicken gizzards in a spicy sauce) and íscas de fígado (fried liver).

restaurants.quote: I usually like to order some codfish cakes (they are the best in town!), octopus salad with green sauce, moelinhas (chicken gizzards in a spicy sauce) and íscas de fígado (fried liver).

restaurants.name: Terra Nova

sources.quote: offers a slightly more polished take on seafood dining, with a strong focus on codfish and oysters
Takes local products and recipes and mixes them with foreign flavors to create an international presentation and evolution of local gastronomy.

restaurants.quote: offers a slightly more polished take on seafood dining, with a strong focus on codfish and oysters

restaurants.name: Restaurante Costume Bistrô

sources.quote: The interior of the restaurant is stunning and you will surely expect crazy high prices when you see it.

restaurants.quote: The interior of the restaurant is stunning and you will surely expect crazy high prices when you see it.

restaurants.name: Casa Expresso

sources.quote: They continue to serve local traditional foods that almost all other restaurants have forgotten about.

This unassuming tavern retains its traditional charm, serving delicacies and snacks at its stainless steel counter, with timeless favorites such as beef liver with onions, cod escabeche, and rojão sandwiches.

restaurants.quote: This unassuming tavern retains its traditional charm, serving delicacies and snacks at its stainless steel counter, with timeless favorites such as beef liver with onions, cod escabeche, and rojão sandwiches.

restaurants.name: Barro

sources.quote: Tânia Durão, 39 anos, e Francisco Marinho, 35, são a dupla por detrás deste novo restaurante, aberto no fim de junho numa artéria pedonal entre as ruas de Cedofeita e de Bombarda.

restaurants.quote: Tânia Durão, 39 anos, e Francisco Marinho, 35, são a dupla por detrás deste novo restaurante, aberto no fim de junho numa artéria pedonal entre as ruas de Cedofeita e de Bombarda.

restaurants.name: Urraca

sources.quote: Autodenomina-se como 'irmã' do Mafalda's (o restaurante no Mercado de Matosinhos, de Inês e Mafalda Pando e Miguel Prata) este Urraca que desde julho serve 'uma comida sazonal e do mundo' no Fiasco.

restaurants.quote: Autodenomina-se como 'irmã' do Mafalda's (o restaurante no Mercado de Matosinhos, de Inês e Mafalda Pando e Miguel Prata) este Urraca que desde julho serve 'uma comida sazonal e do mundo' no Fiasco.

restaurants.name: L'Egoïste

sources.quote: A oliveira no meio da sala dá o mote à carta portuguesa deste novo restaurante 'que quer ser disruptivo e divertido'.

restaurants.quote: A oliveira no meio da sala dá o mote à carta portuguesa deste novo restaurante 'que quer ser disruptivo e divertido'.

restaurants.name: Blss U

sources.quote: A carta, da autoria do chefe Xavier Oliveira com a curadoria do chefe Miguel Rocha Vieira, é inspirada nos sete pecados capitais e pretende ser 'irreverente' à volta da cozinha do mundo.

restaurants.quote: A carta, da autoria do chefe Xavier Oliveira com a curadoria do chefe Miguel Rocha Vieira, é inspirada nos sete pecados capitais e pretende ser 'irreverente' à volta da cozinha do mundo.

restaurants.name: Ikebana Izakaya

sources.quote: Aberta em meados de agosto, a nova taberna japonesa da cidade (com o nome da arte floral japonesa ikebana) tem a assinatura de Agnaldo Ferreira, chefe e sócio dos restaurantes Hikidashi e Ikeda.

restaurants.quote: Aberta em meados de agosto, a nova taberna japonesa da cidade (com o nome da arte floral japonesa ikebana) tem a assinatura de Agnaldo Ferreira, chefe e sócio dos restaurantes Hikidashi e Ikeda.

restaurants.name: Borboleta

sources.quote: Their 'vitello tomato' has become a classic: a rare cut of tender veal paired with a light, delicately acidic tomato sauce.

restaurants.quote: Their 'vitello tomato' has become a classic: a rare cut of tender veal paired with a light, delicately acidic tomato sauce.

restaurants.name: Venn Canteen

sources.quote: Founded by three partners from different parts of the world — Snider (Goa), Monika (Poland), and Julian (Germany) — this vegetarian restaurant features the polenta made with Reinette apples, crisped fresh sage, and charred leeks.

restaurants.quote: Founded by three partners from different parts of the world — Snider (Goa), Monika (Poland), and Julian (Germany) — this vegetarian restaurant features the polenta made with Reinette apples, crisped fresh sage, and charred leeks.

restaurants.name: Soma Café e Cozinha

sources.quote: Run by three Brazilian partners — Manuel Saraiva, Mona Karenina, and Luíza de Almeida — this space offers dishes including egg moqueca, a not-to-miss take on the popular Brazilian stew.

restaurants.quote: Run by three Brazilian partners — Manuel Saraiva, Mona Karenina, and Luíza de Almeida — this space offers dishes including egg moqueca, a not-to-miss take on the popular Brazilian stew.

restaurants.name: Éter

sources.quote: Chef Rúben Santos leads this wine bar, featuring raw beef with tertiary sauce and bone marrow, and grilled broccoli with celery vichyssoise.

restaurants.quote: Chef Rúben Santos leads this wine bar, featuring raw beef with tertiary sauce and bone marrow, and grilled broccoli with celery vichyssoise.

restaurants.name: Liz

sources.quote: Chef Daniel Carvalheira delivers a deeply personal cuisine in an intimate space, with a menu reflecting his Mozambican roots, including prawn curry prepared with haute cuisine techniques.

restaurants.quote: Chef Daniel Carvalheira delivers a deeply personal cuisine in an intimate space, with a menu reflecting his Mozambican roots, including prawn curry prepared with haute cuisine techniques.
